title: Success
summary: What does success really mean? Is it wealth, happiness, or something else entirely? Explore diverse definitions of success and the paths people take to achieve their goals.

questions.text: How do you personally define success? Has your definition changed over the years?
Is success mostly the result of hard work, talent, or luck?
Do you think society pressures people into pursuing a specific type of success (e.g. wealth, status)?
Can someone be successful professionally but a failure in their personal life?
What role does failure play in achieving success?
Do you think the definition of success differs between generations? How?
If you could choose between being famous and being happy, which would you pick? Why?

vocabulary.definition: Continued effort and determination in doing something despite difficulty or failure.
examples: Her perseverance finally paid off after years of rejection.

vocabulary.definition: A significant event or stage in the development or progress of something.
examples: Getting her first published article was a major milestone in her career.

vocabulary.definition: A reversal or check in progress; a problem that delays or prevents something.
examples: The company's bankruptcy was a major setback for his career.

vocabulary.definition: A hope or ambition of achieving something.
examples: His aspiration to become a doctor drove every decision he made.

vocabulary.definition: Satisfaction or happiness as a result of fully developing one's abilities or character.
examples: She found more fulfillment in teaching than in her previous corporate job.

phrases: Success is not a destination, it's a journey.
Behind every success story is a failure story.
The definition of success is personal.
idioms: Go from strength to strength — to keep getting more successful
Make it big — to become very successful
Hit the jackpot — to achieve something very valuable or profitable

quotes.text: Success is not final; failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: The secret of success is to do the common thing uncommonly well.
quotes.author: John D. Rockefeller Jr.

teacher_notes: This advanced topic is ideal for C1 learners in professional or academic contexts. Encourage critical thinking about societal definitions of success versus personal values. The quotes work well as discussion starters.

games.name: Success Ranking
games.description: Give each student a list of success factors: wealth, health, family, fame, freedom, knowledge. In pairs, they rank them and then debate their choices.

games.name: Famous Failure
games.description: The teacher presents famous people who failed before succeeding (J.K. Rowling, Steve Jobs, etc.). Students discuss what makes these stories inspiring.

roleplay.scenario: A young person is torn between a high-paying job they don't love and a low-paying job they're passionate about. Advise them.
